reasoning: Correctly retrieves 386 hanabi festival records from Wikipedia/Wikidata and addresses the fireworks intent. However nearly all items lack prefecture, coordinates, dates, and English descriptions. The agent gets a list but cannot easily help the user plan attendance. Quantity is impressive; quality per-item is thin.
missing_data: Dates/months for each festival (most critical for planning), English names/descriptions for nearly all items, and coordinates for the majority of records. Prefecture coverage also sparse.
improvement_hint: Enrich hanabi records with event month and prefecture at scrape time; add English name via translation pass so the agent can immediately surface a date-filtered shortlist.

reasoning: Identical result set to L3-01 but lang=ja. The Japanese query is served identically: 386 records, same thin metadata. Intent is understood, source is authoritative, but lack of dates and coordinates severely limits practical use for a Japanese speaker planning attendance.
missing_data: Event dates (month/period), coordinates, and Japanese descriptions for most items.
improvement_hint: Same as L3-01: add event month field from Wikipedia article text and coordinates from Wikidata to improve planning utility.

reasoning: Query asks about snow festivals in Japan; tool correctly filters prefecture=Hokkaido but returns 0 festival items. The response then surfaces UNESCO ICH records (Dainichido Bugaku, Hayachine Kagura, Kawagoe Hikawa Festival) which are unrelated to snow festivals and not in Hokkaido. The constraint is explicitly violated. No Sapporo Snow Festival or any yukimatsuri data appears.
missing_data: Sapporo Snow Festival, Asahikawa Winter Festival, and any other snow festival entries. No yuki-matsuri kind_tag seems to exist in the corpus.
wrong_data: National_heritage fallback items (Dainichido Bugaku in Akita, Hayachine Kagura, Kawagoe Festival) are geographically and topically irrelevant to the query.
improvement_hint: Add a yuki/snow festival kind_tag or keyword field; ensure Sapporo Snow Festival is in the corpus. Also suppress national_japan-scoped fallback items when a prefecture filter is active.

reasoning: Query asks for rural lifestyle experience (田舎暮らし体験) in Chinese. Tool calls get_japan_heritage which returns heritage designations — castles, archaeological sites, Dazaifu — none of which match the rural-living intent. The intent mismatch is fundamental. Chinese language is served but top_canonical_heritage items are urban/monumental.
missing_data: Satoyama stays, agricultural experience programs, kominka lodging, rural DMO listings relevant to countryside immersion.
improvement_hint: Improve intent mapping so rural-life queries route to search_hybrid with keywords like 農村 / 農泊 / 里山 or to get_dmo which may have rural-experience listings, rather than heritage monuments.

reasoning: Korean query for autumn leaf spots. search_area returns 146 matches including named parks with 紅葉 in their titles plus famous sakura-meisho locations tagged kinds_class_match. Results are grounded with coordinates and heritage labels. However the top results are mostly parks named '紅葉 something' rather than the best-known koyo spots (Nikko, Arashiyama), limiting usefulness.
missing_data: Famous koyo destinations such as Nikko, Arashiyama/Kyoto, Towada, Shirakami; best-season timing information.
improvement_hint: Add koyo_meisho kind tag to well-known autumn-leaf spots so they surface prominently for this query rather than relying solely on title-match.

reasoning: Query wants quiet meditation, explicitly 'not a touristy temple.' Response surfaces 修行寺 (a temple in Tokyo) and 修行院, then a Shizuoka event for Rinzaiji special viewing, and a ninja experience — diverse but does not filter for non-touristy or quiet. The constraint 'not touristy' is not honoured. One good lead: the Rinzaiji training experience is genuinely relevant.
missing_data: Zen training temples (sesshin, zazen stays), mountain temples with actual meditation programs, less-known rural retreat temples.
improvement_hint: Add a 'shugyō_experience' or 'zazen_stay' kind tag for temples offering retreat programs; surface those over generic tourist temples for meditation queries.

reasoning: Query asks for Northern Lights in Japan — which do not reliably occur in Japan as a tourist attraction. The response returns national parks and an observatory via kinds_class_match, none of which are related to aurora viewing. This is a reasoning failure: the system should inform the agent that auroras are not a reliable Japan tourism product, rather than returning loosely matched results.
missing_data: Honest null/empty response with a note that aurora viewing is not a reliable Japan tourism product; if anything, pointer to northern Hokkaido dark-sky sites.
wrong_data: Okinawa national parks are surfaced, which are warm-climate tropical parks entirely irrelevant to aurora viewing.
improvement_hint: Add a safety/feasibility check: when a query targets a phenomenon not available in Japan (aurora, midnight sun), return an empty result or metadata note rather than tangentially matched records.

reasoning: Query asks for 100-year-old house (古民家) stays in Gifu. Response returns onsen ryokan (Hito-yu-kai members) and generic OSM hotels in Gifu. No kominka/machiya accommodation is returned. While Gifu (Hida/Takayama) is a famous kominka destination, the tool call (get_hotels) does not filter for historic lodging type, and the corpus apparently lacks kominka lodging records.
missing_data: Kominka guesthouses in Shirakawago, Gero, Takayama; machiya stays. A kominka lodging_type or kind tag would help.
improvement_hint: Add kominka / machiya as distinct lodging_type values; scrape or ingest Japan Heritage/Tourism Agency kominka-stay registry data to populate these.

reasoning: Thai query for 'new places not crowded with tourists' — essentially off-beaten-path sakura spots. The tool retrieves 199 sakura-related results including some genuine gems (Matsumae Castle, Sakurayama in Gunma) but does not address the low-crowd constraint at all. Famous crowded spots (Himeji Castle, Kumamoto Castle) appear prominently. Intent partially understood (sakura yes, hidden no).
missing_data: Filtering or tagging for lesser-known sakura-meisho vs. famous crowded ones; seasonal timing for sakura.
improvement_hint: Add a 'regional_only' or 'off_beaten_path' flag based on prominence_score threshold; results with prominence_score < 0.65 could be flagged as less-touristed in the response metadata.

reasoning: Photography / dramatic landscape query. search_area on 絶景 returns 53 results including DMO content from Ehime and Kumamoto with specific landscape lists. Municipal scrape results contain detailed named spots. Content is in Japanese only, which limits the English-speaking agent. Functional but imperfect — English name/description coverage is sparse.
missing_data: English descriptions for most results; iconic landscape spots like Mt. Fuji viewpoints, Biei Blue Pond, Cape Toi, Tottori Dunes.
improvement_hint: Ensure top 絶景 wikidata attractions get English translations; add zekkei kind_tag or sub-category to surface canonical dramatic-landscape spots.

reasoning: Arabic query for outdoor hot springs not in hotels. Response returns 72 露天風呂 results, many being onsen facilities within ryokan/hotels (見奈良天然温泉, etc.) — exactly what the user wants to avoid. A few wild/natural bath entries appear (宝泉坊温泉 has outdoor baths). Constraint 'not in hotels' is not filtered. Results are in Japanese with no Arabic or English descriptions.
missing_data: Outdoor natural 野湯 (unmanaged natural hot springs) or rotenburo accessible without lodging; filtering out hotel-attached baths.
improvement_hint: Add a rotenburo_public or nōyu kind_tag for outdoor hot springs accessible without hotel stay; distinguish from hotel rotenburo in lodging records.

reasoning: Query asks for wild dolphin swimming. Top result is 'Kyoto Tourism Ambassadors' page — completely off-target. Second result is Notojima Aquarium (dolphins in captivity, not wild). Other results are a fish market. None address wild dolphin encounters. The system failed to reason that this query requires ecotourism/marine experience data, not broad イルカ keyword matching.
missing_data: Mikurajima (Tokyo islands) wild dolphin snorkeling; Amami or Ogasawara wild dolphin tours; any official ecotourism dolphin program listings.
wrong_data: Top result (Kyoto Tourism Ambassadors) has no connection to dolphins and appears due to noise in the scrape data.
improvement_hint: Add marine_experience and wildlife_swim kind tags; filter noise spots where description does not confirm the named entity; deduplicate portal pages that aggregate multiple topics.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional architecture districts (重伝建). Tool returns get_japan_heritage with 104 Japan Heritage stories. Top canonical heritage items are castles and archaeological sites — not preserved townscape districts. While Japan Heritage includes some relevant stories, the core data need (重要伝統的建造物群保存地区) is not what this tool returns. Intent partially overlaps but the right data is not surfaced.
missing_data: 重伝建 district list (Shirakawago, Gion Hanamikoji, Ouchijuku, etc.); search_area with preservation_district kind would be better.
improvement_hint: Route 'traditional architecture / townscape' queries to search_area filtering on preservation_district kind or add a dedicated get_preservation_districts tool; the Japan Heritage tool is not the right match.

reasoning: Vietnamese query for Ainu culture in Hokkaido. Returns 125 results with Ainu Museum, National Ainu Museum (Upopoy), Nibutani Ainu Culture Museum, Hokkaido Ainu Center, Kawamura Kaneto Museum — all highly relevant with coordinates and websites. Coverage is excellent; most records are Hokkaido-based as expected. Main gap is lack of Vietnamese descriptions.
missing_data: Vietnamese-language descriptions; Upopoy/Shiraoi cultural village experience details (the national Ainu Museum and Porotokotan village); hands-on craft programs.
improvement_hint: Ensure Vietnamese translation pass covers top Ainu-related records; add 'ainu_culture_experience' kind_tag for venues offering interactive programs.

reasoning: Query about cliff-carved Buddhist statues (磨崖仏). Returns 72 results led by Usuki Stone Buddhas (National Treasure, coordinates) and Oya-dera, Kiyomizu Magaibutsu — exactly the right entities with heritage designations and prominence scores. Coverage spans Oita (most famous), Tochigi, Kagoshima, Fukuoka. Very strong retrieval.
missing_data: English descriptions for several entries; access information and opening hours for top sites.
improvement_hint: Add English translations for top magaibutsu records (臼杵磨崖仏 has none currently) and include visit duration / access notes from DMO scrapes.

reasoning: Query requests day-hike volcanoes from Tokyo. Mt. Fuji appears first (correct), but subsequent results include Lake Towada (Aomori — not day-trippable from Tokyo), Mt. Unzen (Nagasaki), Lake Mashu (Hokkaido), Mt. Chokai (Akita) — none of which are Tokyo day-trip feasible. The proximity/feasibility constraint is not addressed. Search is topically correct but geographically unfocused.
missing_data: Mt. Fuji day-hike details; Izu/Oshima volcano day trips; proximity to Tokyo filter.
improvement_hint: Add a distance-from-hub parameter or region filtering; when a 'day trip from [city]' constraint is detected, surface only results within ~200km or flag those that are not feasible as day trips.

reasoning: Indonesian query for freshly-made tofu at a temple. Results return MAFF GI tofu products, a Goto Islands tofu article, and mountain tofu shops — none at a temple. The 'temple' (kuil) constraint is entirely missed. Some results (金剛山の豆腐まつまさ — at a mountain shrine base) come close but are not temples. Tofu foods are grounded but the experience + location constraint fails.
missing_data: Koya-san temple tofu (高野豆腐 kaiseki at Eko-in or Shukubo), Kyoto Nanzenji yudofu restaurants, Buddhist vegetarian (shojin ryori) venues.
improvement_hint: Map 'tofu at temple' intent to shojin_ryori + buddhist_temple kind intersection; add temple-affiliated dining as a spot subtype in scraper output.

reasoning: Query for fishing villages frozen in time. Returns 漁港 results including Itoman fishing port (Okinawa), Shizuoka sakura ebi harbor dining, and a market in Niigata. One result is a closed-to-public port notice. These are working ports, not preserved villages. Classic destinations like Ine no Funaya (Kyoto) or Tomo no Ura (Hiroshima) do not appear.
missing_data: Ine no Funaya boat houses in Kyoto, Tomo no Ura in Hiroshima, Mihama in Fukui — all designated preserved fishing villages.
improvement_hint: Add funaya / fishing_village_preservation kind tag for designated traditional fishing port districts; a closed-access port notice should not surface as a tourist spot.

reasoning: Query asks where to learn washi paper-making from a master. Tool returns get_local_specialty with category=craft, returning 231 METI craft designations. Results start with Nibutani Attus (Ainu weaving) and Oitama Tsumugi (silk) — no washi results visible in the truncated output. Even if washi appears later, the response fails to connect to hands-on learning locations.
missing_data: Echizen washi (Fukui), Mino washi (Gifu), Ogawa washi (Saitama) — all with visitor workshops. Craft designation records do not link to workshop venues.
improvement_hint: Add workshop_available boolean to METI craft records when the production area has publicly accessible craft-experience venues; or link to DMO experience listings.

reasoning: Query for time-drama-like townscapes. search_area on 宿場 returns Narai-juku, Seki-juku, Tsumago-juku — all Important Preservation Districts with coordinates. Also includes DMO editorial content from Nagano with rich descriptions of Suwarashuku and Yanagimachi. Excellent match. Results are grounded, specific, and practically useful.
missing_data: Some famous juku like Magome or Mariko are not visible in the truncated results; opening-hours / access from major hubs.
improvement_hint: Ensure all 重伝建-designated shukuba-machi (宿場町) have preservation_district and shukuba kind tags; surface them with heritage designation labels for easy agent filtering.

reasoning: Query for uncrowded beaches. Response returns 114 beach records mostly in Okinawa — Naminoue Beach, Sunayama Beach, Tropical Beach — many of which are popular tourist beaches. The 'not crowded' constraint is unaddressed. Dataset has good beach coverage but no mechanism to distinguish crowd level. Hamasaka Kenmin Sun Beach (Hyogo) with hakusha_seishou_100 tag is a reasonable less-known pick.
missing_data: Crowd-level signal or regional diversity beyond Okinawa concentration; mainland beaches in Niigata, Shimane, or Kochi that are genuinely less visited.
improvement_hint: Distribute beach results geographically with prominence_score as a proxy for crowd level; low-prominence beaches with hakusha_seishou_100 tag could be flagged as less-touristed alternatives.

reasoning: Query for old-school izakaya with locals. search_area on 横丁 returns 74 results. First result is 横丁公園 (a park in Tokyo), second is a Saga tourism overview page in Chinese, then Haikara't Yokocho (an amusement park). Genuine yokocho alley districts (Yurakucho, Omoide Yokocho, Namba Sennichimae) are not visible. Relevant data may exist but is buried under noise.
missing_data: Omoide Yokocho (Tokyo), Yurakucho alley, Nishi-Ogikubo and Koenji streets; izakaya district information.
improvement_hint: Boost yokocho-kind wikidata attractions over scrape portals for this query; add izakaya_alley subtype to distinguish drinking alleys from amusement parks with 'yokocho' in the name.

reasoning: German query for rural train travel. Returns 74 results including Sagano Scenic Line (Kyoto), Oigawa Main Line (steam trains, Shizuoka), Oga Line, Ou Main Line, Omato Line — good variety of local railway lines across Japan. Nagano editorial content on Ueda Electric Railway adds rich detail. Mostly lacks German descriptions but data is solid.
missing_data: German descriptions; JR Isan Line, Gono Line (Akita/Aomori) or Akechi Railway as iconic rural railways; schedule and booking hints.
improvement_hint: Ensure local_railway kind records get German translation; add scenic_railway sub-tag for lines specifically noted for scenic routes.

reasoning: Firefly viewing query. Returns 71 results including ホタルの里 park (Hyogo), Firefly Museum (Yamaguchi), Kitakyushu firefly page, Toyoda-cho firefly boat event (very specific with dates and prices), and Kenrokuen firefly viewing event in Kanazawa with 2026 schedule. Useful and seasonally relevant content is present. Some noise (Kanazawa site is an aggregator page).
missing_data: Genji-botaru Natural Monument designation locations; best-month/season guidance (June typically); access routes to top sites.
improvement_hint: Add hotaru_meisho or wildlife_seasonal kind tag; ensure Toyoda-cho boat event and similar time-specific events surface with month metadata so agents can filter by travel dates.

reasoning: Russian query for winter crane viewing. Top result is 出水ツル渡来地 (Izumi crane grounds, Kagoshima) — the single most important site for winter cranes in Japan, with coordinates, Ramsar designation, and crane_wintering kind tag. Also returns 鶴居村 municipality in Hokkaido (Kushiro wetlands, Red-crowned crane habitat) and Kushiro-shitsugen National Park. Good coverage of the two key crane regions.
missing_data: Russian-language descriptions; Tsurui-Ito Red-crowned Crane Sanctuary (Hokkaido) as a named spot; best viewing season (Oct–Mar) metadata.
wrong_data: Several municipality results (鶴田町, 横浜市鶴見区, 舞鶴市) are city districts named with 鶴 kanji and are not crane-viewing spots — noise from character matching.
improvement_hint: Filter municipality results when a wildlife-specific kind (crane_wintering) is already matched; add Russian descriptions to Izumi and Tsurui top entries; include seasonal note in kind metadata.
